seoTitle: How to Add a Link to a Button in Brevo Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to drag a button block into Brevo's email editor, map it to a destination URL, and style it for high-converting calls to action.
intro: A call-to-action button that doesn't link anywhere is a missed conversion opportunity. This guide covers dragging a button block into Brevo's visual editor, mapping it to an external URL or other link type, and styling the text and colors to match your brand. You'll also learn how to check the button renders correctly on mobile before sending.

steps.heading: Add a button block
steps.body: Open your campaign template canvas, pull a Button block from the left elements library, and drop it into your layout.

steps.heading: Edit the button text
steps.body: Double-click the placeholder text inside the button to type your custom call-to-action message.

steps.heading: Set the link destination
steps.body: Open the Link settings panel on the right, choose Type of link, select Absolute link, and paste your destination URL.

steps.heading: Adjust the link target
steps.body: If you want the link to open in a new browser tab instead of the current one, adjust the Link target dropdown accordingly.

steps.heading: Preview and save
steps.body: Use Preview & test to check how the button scales on different devices, then save the template to lock in your changes.

faq.question: Can I link a button to a phone number instead of a URL?
faq.answer: Yes, Brevo's link type options include click-to-call paths in addition to standard absolute web URLs.

faq.question: How do I make a button link open in a new tab?
faq.answer: Adjust the Link target dropdown parameter in the button's settings panel to force it to open a separate browser tab.

faq.question: Why isn't my button link working after I saved it?
faq.answer: Double check that you selected Absolute link as the type and pasted a complete, valid URL including https:// in the link field.

faq.question: Can I style the button color and font?
faq.answer: Yes, the layout properties sidebar lets you customize text strings, font styling, and brand color fills for the button.
